stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**

REQ-01 through REQ-04 are met in the current tree. The prior blocker has been addressed by production code: [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:941) now includes `decision` in `builtin_default_kinds()`, and [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:977) makes `resolve_item_kinds(None)` return those built-ins. The new default-repo integration tests cover `jit item list --kind decision --json`, `item show`, `per:<qualified-id>` resolution, and markdown-only projection in [crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs:78), [crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs:171), and [crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/decision_kind_tests.rs:200).

**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

Pass. The production change stays in the domain item-kind resolver and constructors. Commands continue to orchestrate config/storage plus pure indexing through [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:43) and [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:112). No storage access was added to `domain/`, and no CLI formatting leaked into commands.

**Functional Paradigm**

Pass. The added production code is small and expression-oriented. `builtin_default_kinds()` constructs, sorts, and returns the default set without side effects beyond validation.

**Correctness And Invariants**

Pass for issue behavior. Decisions are projected from markdown through the existing generic `index_items` path; no parallel structured store was added. No file writes, event logging paths, gate transitions, assignee parsing, label parsing, or PID handling were changed.

The issue dependencies are complete in the provided context: `1e1ea81d` and `61b17603` are both `done`.

**Safety**

Pass. No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed change set.

**Testing**

Pass on provided evidence. I did not rerun tests because the issue has a succeeded `cargo-ci` gate. The current post-fix gate run `5cd03eea-6c90-40ee-b6fd-4699f3eaa516` at commit `e1a534e9` passed with `fmt: ok`, `clippy: ok`, and `test: 2411 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored`.

**Error Handling**

Pass. New fallible constructors return `Result<_, ItemError>`, and no `unwrap()`/`expect()` was added to non-test library code.

**CLI Contract**

Pass for behavior. The new tests exercise `--json` for `item list` and `item show`; [crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs:91) also updates the old `decision` probe to reflect that it is now recognized as a built-in kind.

**Documentation**

Fail. The implementation introduces stale user-facing and public API documentation, which is technical debt under the review standard.

- [crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160) still says only `requirement` is the built-in default for item commands. That is now false; `decision` is also built in.
- [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:41) says item kind resolution falls back to the built-in `requirement` kind when none is declared. That is now false.
- [crates/jit/src/config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:466), [crates/jit/src/config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:821), and [crates/jit/src/config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:1775) also still describe the no-`[item_kinds]` path as requirement-only.

These are not cosmetic only: they document the public CLI/config contract and now contradict the shipped behavior.

**Prior Review Feedback**

Addressed. The previous failed review said the change was test-only and did not configure `decision` in product behavior. That is fixed by [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:941) and covered by default-repo tests.

**Jit Issue Management**

`cargo-ci` passed. I did not find provided evidence for `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`, and I did not rerun it because the review instructions prioritized the succeeded cargo-ci gate for test evidence. Label warnings would be allowed.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
